result:
queue = point-to-point, one consumer gets each message, work distribution
topic = pub/sub, every subscriber gets a copy, fan-out

use queue when:
- job processing, load balancing across workers
- each message must be handled exactly once
- order matters within a single consumer group
- backpressure — slow consumers shouldn't drop work

use topic when:
- multiple independent systems need same event (audit log + search indexer + cache invalidator)
- broadcasting state changes
- decoupling producers from unknown/changing set of consumers
- consumers can be added later without touching producer

modern brokers blur line: Kafka topics with consumer groups act like queues, SNS→SQS fan-out gives you both.
rule of thumb — ask "does this message belong to one worker or to N interested parties?"
